description:
You are a machine-learning engineer who wants to checkpoint the **exact NumPy version** that is currently available in the working environment so it can be reproduced later.  
  
  Your goal is to create a **single, one-line terminal command** that  
    1. Ensures the directory  /home/user/training_env  exists (create it if necessary), and  
    2. Writes a text file  /home/user/training_env/pinned_requirements.txt  whose **only** content is a single line of the form  
  
         numpy==<version-number>  
  
     followed by a trailing newline character (`\n`).  
     • There must be **no leading or trailing spaces**.  
     • There must be **nothing else** in the file—exactly one line.  
     • The `<version-number>` token must be the NumPy version reported by  
          `pip show numpy`  
       at the moment your command is executed.  
  
  Example of the required file content (the exact version may differ):  
  
      numpy==1.24.2\n  
  
  Constraints & tips  
  • Accomplish everything (directory creation + file creation/writing) with **one composite command line** (you may chain shell primitives with `&&`, `;`, pipes, subshells, etc.).  
  • Do **not** modify or install any packages—just read the currently installed NumPy version and record it.  
  • When you are finished, you can verify manually by running  
        cat /home/user/training_env/pinned_requirements.txt  
    which should output exactly the single line described above.  
  
  The automated tests will:  
    • Check that the file /home/user/training_env/pinned_requirements.txt exists.  
    • Confirm that its sole line matches the exact pattern `numpy==X.Y.Z` where *X.Y.Z* is the version string retrieved from `pip show numpy`.  
    • Verify that the file ends with a single newline character and contains no extra whitespace or additional lines.
truth:
Initial system state for verification:  
    • NumPy is already installed site-wide for the user at version 1.24.2.  
      (Command `pip show numpy | grep ^Version` outputs `Version: 1.24.2`)  
  
  Expected final state after successful task completion:  
    • Directory /home/user/training_env exists (permissions 0755 or more permissive for the user).  
    • File /home/user/training_env/pinned_requirements.txt exists (permissions 0644 or more permissive for the user).  
    • The file contains exactly the text  
          numpy==1.24.2\n  
      where `\n` denotes a single newline character.  
    • The file contains no additional lines, no carriage returns (`\r`), and no leading or trailing spaces.
